title: Indulgent Chinese Kung Pao Chicken
description: A resinous, chewy, lacto-vegetarian-friendly dish featuring makrut lime leaves, perfect for a date night. This Kung Pao chicken recipe combines the perfect balance of spicy, sour, and savory flavors.
instructions: Heat 2 tablespoons of oil in a wok over high heat until it reaches 400°F (200°C).
Add the chicken to the wok and cook for 3-4 minutes, until browned and cooked through.
Remove the chicken from the wok and set it aside.
Add the peanuts to the wok and cook for 1-2 minutes, until fragrant and lightly toasted.
Add the garlic, ginger, and Sichuan peppercorns to the wok and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the bhuna chili paste, soy sauce, and hoisin sauce to the wok and cook for 1 minute, until the sauce is bubbly and slightly thickened.
Add the makrut lime leaves and scallions to the wok and cook for 1 minute, until the leaves are slightly wilted.
Return the chicken to the wok and stir to combine with the sauce.
Cook for an additional 1-2 minutes, until the chicken is coated in the sauce and heated through.
Transfer the chicken to a tagliere and serve immediately.
Serve the chicken hot over steamed rice, garnished with additional scallions and makrut lime leaves if desired.
